title: Assassin’s Creed® III

notes: It seems that new proton update made this game playable. Runs at 60fps no problems. I also recommend to disable uplay overlay because when you run any uplay game with it on it will show the(shift + f2) message without ever hiding.
title: Assassin’s Creed® III

notes: Runs perfectly out of the box. The only thing that bugs me is the fact that in game overlay message doesn't go away, but you can solve it by just disabling it inside uplay settings.
title: Assassin’s Creed® III

notes:
It installs and runs out of the box with latest proton update. It works fine between 60-144 FPS with Esync disabled (depending on the scene - 60 FPS in open spaces - 144 FPS for inside environments). 
It seems some surface are transparent but shouldn't, like chairs tissues in the Opera or some tissues on clothes. These problems are minimal.

Video of first Assassination: https://youtu.be/frJ8l2UjjkQ

title: Assassin’s Creed® III

notes: Everything runs perfectly on first start. No setup. Once you get in, performance is super choppy. Disabling Esync makes the game run absolutely perfectly. Recommended to "Remember Me" on uPlay login. Also recommended to disable in-game overlay, notifications, etc in uPlay settings.
title: Assassin’s Creed® III

notes: Game crashes on start with uplay overlay enabled.
title: Assassin’s Creed® III

notes: Runs fine once you disable the Uplay overlay. Frame rate is variable, but cranking the resolution down a few notches helps. No graphical issues, audio glitches or other weirdness... well, none except those that shipped with the game. Totally playable.
title: Assassin’s Creed® III

notes: Played through main story, no noticeable issues. Biggest issue was that UPlay stays open after you quit the game, but that's not really an issue with the game.
title: Assassin’s Creed® III

notes:
With proton 4.x branch, the game installs without additional tweaks. Finally, UPlay installs, logins and even synchronize saved games between two Linux computers.

 The game was tested on a NVidia Optimus laptop with a 1050 Ti card, that require to build the 32 and 64-bit versions of primus_vk to enable DXVK on hybrid GPU systems. It is necessary to install all Optimus, Prime, Vulkan  and OpenGL libraries, both 32 and 64-bit, except the driver of course, before build primus_vk.

Once done, use: pvkrun %command%
title: Assassin’s Creed® III

notes: disable in-game overlay and notifications, runs has it should, one play through for the main story, couple of crashes, PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%
title: Assassin’s Creed® III
